---
title: "Case 4: $B_s^0\\to J/\\psi\\, p\\bar p$"
---

**Status:** <span class="badge-checked">prior tensor check</span> — the
effective-C operator statement and chain-level derivation remain to be
documented here.

## Setting

Weak decay: $\bar b\to \bar c c\bar s$ via internal W emission produces the
charmonium; the $p\bar p$ pair is created from the vacuum by gluons. Chains:

- $P_{c\bar c}\to J/\psi\, p$ (pentaquark) with spectator $\bar p$,
- $\bar P_{c\bar c}\to J/\psi\, \bar p$ with spectator $p$,
- $X\to p\bar p$ with spectator $J/\psi$.

Because the decay is weak, C invariance does **not** force the
$P_{c\bar c}\,\bar p$ and $\bar P_{c\bar c}\,p$ couplings to be related.
Nonetheless a relation *appears to hold*, traced to the production mechanism:
the $p\bar p$ pair originates from C-even, P-even gluonic operators, and the
weak part of the amplitude does not distinguish the two chains.

## What must be established

1. **The precise statement of the effective symmetry.** At which level does
   it hold — operator level (factorization of the weak vertex × gluonic
   $p\bar p$ creation), or only for specific Dirac structures? What breaks
   it (e.g. non-factorizable contributions), i.e. is it a model assumption
   to be quoted, rather than a theorem?
2. **The resulting coupling relation** between $P_{c\bar c}$ and
   $\bar P_{c\bar c}$ chains, with all phases: current-level C phases for
   the baryon pair and the alignment phases from mapping the conjugate-chain
   kinematics to the reference configuration.
3. **Consistency with case 3:** the relation should be the restriction of
   the exact-C result for $J/\psi\to\eta p\bar p$ (with the $\eta$ replaced
   by the weak "source"), which is why case 3 is done first.

## Previous experience

Symmetrizing $p\leftrightarrow\bar p$ here was tricky; the construction was
validated by comparison with tensor amplitudes. Those notes and scripts
should be collected into this folder and re-expressed in the fixed-frame
language:

- fixed-frame statement of the $p\leftrightarrow\bar p$ relation (this is a
  *C-type* species swap, not an identical-particle swap — no statistics
  sign, but fermion-pair C phases);
- chain-level relation with alignment phases;
- tensor bridge rerun with registry conventions.

## To do

1. Import and re-derive the old result; record the exact coupling relation.
2. Write the "effective C" argument with explicit operator structures
   ($p\bar p$ from $G_{\mu\nu}$ bilinears) and its limitations.
3. Numerical battery (conjugation test restricted to the subsystem).
4. CP: relation to $\bar B_s^0\to J/\psi\, p\bar p$ — same final state!
   Untangling direct and mixing-induced structure is beyond pure
   symmetrization but the amplitude relations feed into it.
